ddls:
CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the semiconductor device */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the device */,
  "date_introduced" TEXT /* Date the device was introduced */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of the device */,
  "device_specifications" TEXT /* Detailed specifications of the device */,
  "last_test_date" TEXT /* Date of the last test conducted on the device */,
  "next_test_date" TEXT /* Scheduled date for the next test */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_parameters" (
  "param_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each parameter */,
  "param_name" TEXT /* Name of the electrical parameter */,
  "symbol" TEXT /* Symbol representing the parameter */,
  "units" TEXT /* Units for the parameter measurement */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the parameter */,
  "criticality" TEXT /* Importance level of the parameter */,
  "default_min_value" REAL /* Default minimum acceptable value */,
  "default_max_value" REAL /* Default maximum acceptable value */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("param_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_conditions" (
  "condition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test condition */,
  "condition_description" TEXT /* Description of the test condition */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environmental conditions for the test */,
  "duration" TEXT /* Duration of the test condition */,
  "setup_instructions" TEXT /* Instructions for setting up the test */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("condition_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test result */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device tested */,
  "param_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parameter tested */,
  "condition_id" INTEGER /* ID of the condition under which the test was performed */,
  "result_value" REAL /* Value obtained from the test */,
  "min_value" REAL /* Minimum acceptable value */,
  "max_value" REAL /* Maximum acceptable value */,
  "recorded_date" TEXT /* Date the result was recorded */,
  "test_session_id" INTEGER /* ID of the test session */,
  "engineer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the engineer conducting the test */,
  "test_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments on the test */,
  "test_status" TEXT /* Status of the test */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_condition_id FOREIGN KEY ("condition_id") REFERENCES test_conditions ("condition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_test_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("test_session_id") REFERENCES test_sessions ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_engineer_id FOREIGN KEY ("engineer_id") REFERENCES engineers ("engineer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_param_id FOREIGN KEY ("param_id") REFERENCES test_parameters ("param_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_results_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "measurement_units" (
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each measurement unit */,
  "unit_name" TEXT /* Name of the unit (e.g., V, nA, µA) */,
  "conversion_factor" REAL /* Conversion factor to a base unit */,
  "unit_type" TEXT /* Type of measurement */,
  "unit_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the unit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "engineers" (
  "engineer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each engineer */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the engineer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the engineer */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the engineer in the testing process */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the engineer belongs to */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level for the engineer */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact number for the engineer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engineer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test session */,
  "engineer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the engineer conducting the tests */,
  "session_date" TEXT /* Date of the test session */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the test session is conducted */,
  "test_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments on the session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "quality_control" (
  "qc_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each quality control check */,
  "result_id" INTEGER /* ID of the corresponding test result */,
  "qc_passed" INTEGER /* Indicator if the quality control check passed */,
  "qc_date" TEXT /* Date of the quality control check */,
  "qc_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments on the quality control check */,
  "qc_engineer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the engineer conducting the quality control check */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("qc_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_quality_control_result_id FOREIGN KEY ("result_id") REFERENCES test_results ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_quality_control_qc_engineer_id FOREIGN KEY ("qc_engineer_id") REFERENCES engineers ("engineer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "standards" (
  "standard_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each standard */,
  "standard_name" TEXT /* Name of the standard */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the standard */,
  "standard_version" TEXT /* Version of the standard */,
  "standard_date" TEXT /* Date the standard was published */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("standard_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_standards" (
  "test_standard_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test-standard relationship */,
  "param_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parameter being tested */,
  "standard_id" INTEGER /* ID of the standard to which it must comply */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_standard_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_standards_param_id FOREIGN KEY ("param_id") REFERENCES test_parameters ("param_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_standards_standard_id FOREIGN KEY ("standard_id") REFERENCES standards ("standard_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_metadata" (
  "metadata_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the metadata entry */,
  "result_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated test result */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the metadata entry */,
  "metadata_type" TEXT /* Type of metadata */,
  "metadata_source" TEXT /* Source of the metadata */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metadata_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_metadata_result_id FOREIGN KEY ("result_id") REFERENCES test_results ("result_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "units_conversion" (
  "conversion_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the conversion entry */,
  "from_unit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the source unit */,
  "to_unit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the target unit */,
  "conversion_factor" REAL /* Factor to convert from source unit to target unit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("conversion_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_units_conversion_to_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("to_unit_id") REFERENCES measurement_units ("unit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_units_conversion_from_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("from_unit_id") REFERENCES measurement_units ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "result_id" INTEGER /* ID of the associated test result */,
  "log_entry" TEXT /* Details about the log entry */,
  "entry_date" TEXT /* Date the log entry was made */,
  "log_type" TEXT /* Type of log */,
  "log_source" TEXT /* Source of the log */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_test_logs_result_id FOREIGN KEY ("result_id") REFERENCES test_results ("result_id")
)
